name: Kenny Washington
abstract: Kenny Washington (born May 29, 1958) is an American jazz drummer and music writer born in Staten Island, New York. His brother is bassist Reggie Washington.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Music Workshop of Staten Island/ Hilton Ruiz Quartet
JVC Jazz Festival: Jazzmobile Salute to Dr. George Butler
Carnegie Hall Salutes the Jazz Masters: Verve Records at 50

works.t: No No Nanette: Tea for Two

The First Time Ever I Saw Your Face

about_quotes.speaker: Randy Sandke
about_quotes.evidence: I financed my own first record... and hired, got Mike Brecker and Jim McNealy and John Goldsby, Kenny Washington and did all originals in a modern context.
about_quotes.doc_title: Randy Sandke interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 10, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Charlap
about_quotes.evidence: Well my trio is Kenny Washington and Peter Washington. And we’ve been playing together for the last two and a half years or so.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Charlap interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 16, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: John Pizzarelli, Jr.
about_quotes.evidence: Harry Allen is one of my dearest friends. He’s a great tenor saxophone player. And he’ll go out and work with Bill Charlap and Kenny Washington and Peter Washington
about_quotes.doc_title: John Pizzarelli, Jr. interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 29, 2000 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: people who are say forty and under haven’t bothered to get what Kenny Washington called pre-bop
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Wilson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 25, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Vincent Pelote
about_quotes.evidence: That happened in a Benny Carter session actually with Kenny Washington playing drums.
about_quotes.doc_title: Vincent Pelote interviewed by Monk Rowe, Orlando, Florida, February 25, 2017

about_quotes.speaker: Richard Wyands
about_quotes.evidence: Most of my records I’ve used Peter Washington and Kenny Washington.
about_quotes.doc_title: Richard Wyands interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 7, 2002
